executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised in the last 48 hours. Major victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, National Money Mart (database leaked), and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group alone has claimed 20 victims, while Clop has targeted 14 Canadian organizations including law firms and technology companies. Critical infrastructure sectors hit include healthcare, education, financial services, and public sector entities, indicating a coordinated or opportunistic mass exploitation event targeting Canadian organizations specifically.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20 Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Eastern Townships School Board, Questica, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), and multiple law/accounting firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 14 Canadian victims including strategic objectives consultancy, ideal welders, CMH Helicopters, law firms (SMT Lawyers), technology companies (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Technikel), and Whiskijack Resorts
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 9 Canadian victims including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Security ONE Alarm Systems, and accounting firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a severe ransomware crisis with 100+ organizations compromised across all sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure victims include Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, and the Eastern Townships School Board. No CCCS advisories were reported in the feed, suggesting potential information lag during an active mass-compromise event.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest ransomware group), Morison Insurance Brokers (hit by Sinobi)

report_2w.executive_summary: The Canadian government issued an alert for CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway products, requiring immediate attention. Two major supply chain attacks dominated the period: North Korean-linked UNC1069 compromised the widely-used Axios npm package to deploy remote access trojans, and the TeamPCP campaign breached the European Commission cloud infrastructure via a Trivy tool compromise, exposing data from 30 EU entities. Additionally, a TrueConf zero-day is being actively exploited in Asian government attacks, and critical vulnerabilities in Cisco IMC (CVE-2026-20093) and Progress ShareFile enable unauthenticated remote takeover. Ransomware groups Qilin and Akira continue active campaigns, and pro-Iran Handala group breached Israeli defense contractors.

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.software_affected: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway

items.description: Critical Cisco IMC authentication bypass allowing unauthenticated admin access to UCS servers
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: UNC1069 (suspected North Korean nexus)
items.targets: Axios npm package maintainers, JavaScript/Node.js development supply chain
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), social engineering of open-source maintainers, deployment of remote access trojans (RAT)

items.name: TeamPCP (ShinyHunters affiliated)
items.targets: European Commission AWS cloud infrastructure, 30 EU entities, Sportradar, 1,000+ SaaS environments
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools), T1199 (Trusted Relationship), Trivy security scanner compromise, CI/CD pipeline abuse, tag poisoning, credential theft

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), ransomware deployment

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-006 on March 30, 2026, warning of CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway products, requiring immediate patching by Canadian organizations. No specific incidents targeting Canadian critical infrastructure, financial institutions, or major retailers were identified in this reporting period. Canadian organizations should be aware of the global supply chain attacks (Axios npm, Trivy/TeamPCP) that could impact domestic development pipelines and SaaS environments.
